prevent the possibility of industrial action.

phone was faulty.

content.text:

Borrow money on a short-term bank loan
Take on a new mortgage to purchase buildings
Offer a discount to customers for early payment

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | ------- |
|  - Outlines a potential issue this business could face in relation to both cost and quality of outputs | 2 |
|  - Provides some relevant information  | 1 |

answer.summary: The business can reduce the costs of inputs when they source globally, cheaper inputs such as rubber, may result in lower quality tyres.

content.text:
Outline ONE potential issue this business could face in relation to both cost and
quality of outputs.

| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | ------- |
|  - Explains the benefits of achieving economies of scale for both the
business and consumers | 3 |
|  - Describes the benefit(s) of achieving economies of scale for the business
and/or consumers  | 2 |
| -  Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary:
Economies of scale will provide this business with cost advantages by increasing the volume of its production, allowing the business to lower the price of the finished product. Consumers will benefit from the reduced price and the business will then increase sales and profits in the
long term.

content.text:
Explain the benefits of achieving economies of scale for both the business and
consumers.

| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | ------- |
| - Provides a thorough explanation of why staff and management in this
business might be resistant to this change | 5 |
| - Provides a sound explanation of why staff and management in this
business might be resistant to this change | 4 |
|  - Demonstrates some understanding of why staff and/or management in
this business might be resistant to this change  | 3 |
|  - Identifies why staff and/or management in this business might be
resistant to this change  | 2 |
| -  Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary:
The leading-edge technology purchased would be costly for management. The new technology may also require staff and management to be retrained. This can be expensive for management and staff may be resistant to learning new techniques due to fear or inertia.
Fear of job loss may cause resistance to change as staff may feel that processes may become automated and their jobs may become redundant. This also imposes extra costs to the business.

content.text: Explain why both staff and management might be resistant to this change.

stimulus.text:
The owners of the tyre manufacturing business have purchased leading-edge
technology to help achieve economies of scale.

An Australian tyre manufacturer is expanding its business. In order to reduce operating costs and increase sales and profit, rubber will be sourced from the global market. 

The business wants to:
- maintain quality outputs
- achieve economies of scale.

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Provides the purpose of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) legislation | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: It ensures that the best qualified person for the job gets the position and that employers do not hire or reject candidates based on factors such as culture, ethnicity, gender or age.

content.text: What is the purpose of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) legislation?

| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Outlines TWO human resource issues found in the advertisement | 3 |
| Outlines an issue found in the advertisement | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: Superannuation is a legal requirement that all employees are entitled to under minimum standards. Under the Fair Work Act (2009), it is illegal to prohibit union membership in Australia. This business has breached these two requirements in its advertisement.

content.text: Outline TWO human resource issues found in this advertisement.

stimulus.text: The advertisement for a store manager is shown.

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Provides a thorough explanation of the social influences that have contributed to the changing labour force participation rates over the 40 years | 5 |
| Provides a sound explanation of the social influences that have contributed to the changing labour force participation rates over the 40 years | 4 |
| Demonstrates some understanding of the social influences that have contributed to the changing labour force participation rates over the 40 years | 3 |
| Demonstrates some understanding of labour force participation and/or social influences | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: The labour force participation rate for females increased from 44% in 1981 to 62% in 2021, while the male rate decreased from 78% to 71%. Social influences include changing gender roles, greater acceptance of women in the workforce, increased participation of males in child-rearing, higher education completion for females, childcare opportunities, changes in government policy, workplace flexibility, and postponed retirement due to the high cost of living.

content.text:
Explain the social influences that have contributed to the changing labour force
participation rates in the period 1981−2021.

stimulus.text:
The table shows labour force participation rates for males and females in
Australia, 1981−2021.

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Outlines the current product life cycle stage | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: The cereal manufacturer is in the decline stage of the product life cycle. Sales are dropping and the manufacturer is attempting to renew consumer interest by changing the packaging. Post-maturity could also be accepted.

content.text: Outline the current product life cycle stage of this cereal.

| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Demonstrates a clear understanding of how the niche market approach could increase sales for this manufacturer | 3 |
| Demonstrates features of a niche market and/or how this approach could increase sales for the manufacturer | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: A niche market approach would allow the manufacturer to meet the needs of health-conscious customers by offering a healthy range of cereals, such as low-sugar or sugar-free cereals. This targeted approach could increase sales by differentiating the product from competitors, achieving repeat sales and creating customer loyalty.

stimulus.text: How could a *niche market approach* increase sales for this cereal manufacturer?

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Provides comprehensive support for the use of ONE global marketing strategy to expand into this global market | 5 |
| Provides sound support for the use of ONE global marketing strategy to expand into this global market | 4 |
| Demonstrates some understanding of the use of ONE global marketing strategy with some reference to this global market | 3 |
| Demonstrates some understanding of global marketing strategy | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: Customisation is a global marketing strategy where a business tailors the marketing mix for a product sold around the world to individual markets. Customising cereal packaging, such as changing the language used on the cereal box, would help global consumers understand the product's features. Customisation is recommended because it allows the manufacturer to meet the wants and needs of customers in unique global markets, supporting long-term profitability. Other acceptable strategies include standardisation, global branding, competitive positioning and global pricing.

content.text:
Justify ONE global marketing strategy this cereal manufacturer could use to
expand into the global market.

stimulus.text: A cereal manufacturer has decided to change the packaging of its product in an attempt to reverse a drop in sales and remain competitive with existing businesses. It is also considering growing its target market by catering for health-conscious consumers in Australia and eventually expanding into the global market.

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Provides the purpose of a cash flow statement | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: The purpose of a cash flow statement is to identify the inflows and outflows of cash over a period of time.

content.text: What is the purpose of a cash flow statement?

| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Explains a conflict between one short-term and one long-term financial objective | 3 |
| Outlines a conflict between one short-term and one long-term financial objective | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: There may be a conflict between the short-term financial objective of profitability and the long-term objective of growth. A business entering new markets may need new equipment and more staff, causing an initial short-term financial loss while supporting long-term profitability.

content.text:
Explain a possible conflict between ONE short-term and ONE long-term
financial objective.

| Criteria | Marks |
| -------- | -----: |
| Provides a detailed explanation of TWO limitations of financial reports | 5 |
| Provides a sound explanation of TWO limitations of financial reports | 4 |
| Demonstrates some limitations of financial reports | 3 |
| Demonstrates some understanding of limitation(s) of financial reports | 2 |
| Provides some relevant information | 1 |

answer.summary: Valuing assets can be problematic because asset values change over time and valuation can be subjective. For example, land values may increase, meaning the balance sheet understates the net value of the business. Capitalising expenses involves recording an expense as an asset on the balance sheet rather than as an expense in the income statement, which can make net income appear higher and mislead potential investors. Other accepted limitations include normalised earnings, timing issues, debt repayments and notes to the financial statements.

content.text: Explain TWO limitations of financial reports.

| Criteria | Marks | | --- | ---: | | Provides a comprehensive outline of the challenges including the global factors of costs, skills and supply in relation to recruiting and retaining staff.

Provides a comprehensive comparison of the use of debt and equity finance.

Provides a comprehensive recommendation to the business for ONE source of finance to fund the expansion.

Makes effective use of the information provided, demonstrating extensive knowledge and understanding relevant to the question.

Presents a sustained, logical and cohesive business report integrating relevant business terminology and concepts. | 17-20 | | Provides a substantial outline of the global factors of costs, skill and supply in relation to recruiting and/or retaining staff.

Provides a clear comparison of the use of debt and equity finance.

Provides a sound recommendation to the business for ONE source of finance to fund the expansion.

Makes sound use of the information provided, demonstrating knowledge and understanding relevant to the question.

Presents a logical and cohesive business report integrating relevant business terminology and concepts. | 13-16 | | Provides a sound outline of the global factors of costs and/or skills and/or supply in relation to recruiting and/or retaining staff.

Provides a sound comparison of debt and equity finance.

Provides a recommendation to the business for ONE source of finance to fund the expansion.

Makes use of the information provided, demonstrating some knowledge and understanding relevant to the question.

Includes features of a business report and uses some business terminology and concepts. | 9-12 | | Provides some outline of global costs and/or skills and/or supply.

Makes a statement about debt and/or equity finance.

Makes a statement about a possible source of finance.

Includes some features of a business report and uses some basic business terminology and/or concepts. | 5-8 | | Makes some reference to human resource strategies and/or finance.

Uses basic business terminology. | 1-4 |

answer.summary: Answers could include strategies in human resource management, specifically global factors of costs, skills and supply; debt and equity finance, including advantages and disadvantages of each; and influences on financial management. Relevant finance content includes internal sources such as retained profits and external sources such as debt (overdraft, commercial bills, factoring, mortgage, debentures, unsecured notes, leasing) and equity (shares, new issues, rights issues, placements, share purchase plans and private equity).

content.text:
The directors have asked you to write a business report in which you:
- outline some of the challenges including the global factors of costs, skills and supply the
business may need to consider in recruiting and retaining staff
- compare the use of debt and equity as sources of finance
- recommend ONE source of finance this business could use to fund its expansion.

stimulus.text:
Future Century Gaming Pty Ltd operates an online gaming platform targeting players aged 18−40 in Australia. It has been in operation since 2012, providing subscription access to a variety of online games.

The business is considering expanding into emerging global markets but is finding it challenging to recruit and retain skilled, qualified game designers and developers in Australia. It is considering international labour markets to overcome staff shortages.

The business has decided to finance its expansion into global markets through
external sources.

content.text:
| Criteria | Marks | | --- | ---: | | Makes a detailed and informed judgement of the extent to which influences on marketing affect business success.

Applies relevant case study/studies and contemporary business issues.

Presents a sustained, logical and cohesive response and communicates clearly using relevant business terminology and concepts. | 17-20 | | Makes a clear judgement of the extent to which influences on marketing affect business success.

Uses relevant case study/studies and contemporary business issues.

Presents a logical and cohesive response using relevant business terminology and concepts. | 13-16 | | Makes some judgement of the extent to which influences on marketing affect business success.

Makes reference to case study/studies and contemporary business issues.

Communicates using business terminology and concepts. | 9-12 | | Makes statements or generalised comment(s) about influences on marketing and/or business success.

Makes limited reference to case study/studies and/or contemporary business issues.

Communicates using some business terminology and/or concepts. | 5-8 | | Makes limited reference to marketing.

May identify case study/studies.

Uses basic business terminology and/or concepts. | 1-4 |

answer.summary:
Influences on marketing
-  Factors influencing customer choice
    - psychological, sociocultural, economic, government
- Consumer laws
    - deceptive and misleading advertising
    - price discrimination
    - implied conditions
    - warranties
- Ethical
    - truth, accuracy in good taste in advertising, products that may damage health,
    - engaging in fair competition, sugging.

content.text: To what extent do influences on marketing affect business success?

| Criteria | Marks | | --- | ---: | | Makes a detailed and informed judgement of the extent to which influences on operations affect business success.

Applies relevant case study/studies and contemporary business issues.

Presents a sustained, logical and cohesive response and communicates clearly using relevant business terminology and concepts. | 17-20 | | Makes a clear judgement of the extent to which influences on operations affect business success.

Uses relevant case study/studies and contemporary business issues.

Presents a logical and cohesive response using relevant business terminology and concepts. | 13-16 | | Makes some judgement of the extent to which influences on operations affect business success.

Makes reference to case study/studies and contemporary business issues.

Communicates using business terminology and concepts. | 9-12 | | Makes statements or generalised comment(s) about influences on operations and/or business success.

Makes limited reference to case study/studies and/or contemporary business issues.

Communicates using some business terminology and/or concepts. | 5-8 | | Makes limited reference to operations.

May identify case study/studies.

Uses basic business terminology and/or concepts. | 1-4 |

answer.summary: Answers could include influences on operations such as globalisation, technology, quality expectations, cost-based competition, government policies, legal regulation and environmental sustainability. They could also include corporate social responsibility, including the difference between legal compliance and ethical responsibility, environmental sustainability and social responsibility.

content.text: To what extent do influences on operations affect business success?
